Describe the feature

It would be helpful to add canonical 1D models of the solar chromosphere to SunPy. Models that would be helpful to include are those of [Vernazza, Avrett, and Loeser (1981)](https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/1981ApJS...45..635V/abstract) (especially VAL C) and [Avrett & Loeser (2008)](https://iopscience.iop.org/article/10.1086/523671/meta). There are probably other 1D chromospheric models that would be useful to have also.

These models are widely used in solar physics, but often have to be extracted from long tables in the papers.

### Proposed solution

These would probably fit in `sunpy.sun.models`.

It should be noted in the documentation that VAL C is superseded by one of the models in Avrett & Loeser (2008). ### Summary of Changes
This pull request updates the `.github/workflows/sub_package_update.yml` file to automatically add the "backport 6.0" label to pull requests created by the workflow. This label helps to ensure that any necessary updates are backported to the relevant branches.

### Issue Addressed
No specific issue was raised for this change, but it addresses the need to streamline the backporting process for template updates.

### Motivation and Context
Automating the addition of the "backport 6.0" label will ensure that template changes are consistently backported, reducing the risk of missing important updates and improving overall workflow efficiency.

### Additional Notes
- The workflow now includes the `labels` input in the `peter-evans/create-pull-request` action.
- Please review the changes and let me know if any adjustments are needed.

PR Description

### Summary of Changes
This pull request updates the `.github/workflows/sub_package_update.yml` file to automatically add the "backport 6.0" label to pull requests created by the workflow. This label helps to ensure that any necessary updates are backported to the relevant branches.

### Issue Addressed
No specific issue was raised for this change, but it addresses the need to streamline the backporting process for template updates.

### Motivation and Context
Automating the addition of the "backport 6.0" label will ensure that template changes are consistently backported, reducing the risk of missing important updates and improving overall workflow efficiency.

### Additional Notes
- The workflow now includes the `labels` input in the `peter-evans/create-pull-request` action.
- Please review the changes and let me know if any adjustments are needed.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27074","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27074/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27074/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27074/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/27074","id":2532130220,"node_id":"I_kwDOAAnGFs6W7T2s","number":27074,"title":"Sum ignores an undetermined value","user":{"login":"sorrge","id":4583935,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ1ODM5MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4583935?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge","html_url":"https://github.com/sorrge","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sorrge/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-17T20:49:11Z","updated_at":"2024-09-19T15:01:29Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"When I have a sum which gives an undetermined expression for some n:
`rrr2 = sp.Sum((-exp(I*pi*m)*exp(-I*pi*n) + exp(-I*pi*m)*exp(I*pi*n))/(m - n), (n, -sp.oo, sp.oo))`

where m, n are integers, simplify() ignores the m = n case and simplifies everything to 0:
`simplify(rrr2)` -> 0

This should be NaN, because for all m != n the function is 0, for n = m it's 0/0.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27074/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27074/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2361266179","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/27074#issuecomment-2361266179","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27074","id":2361266179,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAAnGFs6MvhAD","user":{"login":"advait-zx","id":144479092,"node_id":"U_kgDOCJyTdA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/144479092?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx","html_url":"https://github.com/advait-zx","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advait-zx/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-19T15:01:27Z","updated_at":"2024-09-19T15:01:27Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"can i work on it","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2361266179/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Provide a general description of the issue or problem.

Having a new topic guide for the eclipse would be nice to be able to advertise SunPy and what SunPy can provide to support the eclipse that is occurring in April 2024. We could create a press release and share the link which would feature code examples and gallery examples. Some content already exists in the [solar-eclipse repo](https://github.com/sunpy/solar-eclipse). It needs to be updated and content migrated. This repo could then be made private or deleted.

Some additional content that could be added

- [ ] A recreation an eclipse path map such as [this one](https://svs.gsfc.nasa.gov/5073).
- [ ] A guide on how to import a photograph into a Map and determine its coordinates
- [ ] Example processing steps to be performed on an eclipse photograph like stacking, flat-fielding, and sharpening

Provide a general description of the issue or problem.

Having a new topic guide for the eclipse would be nice to be able to advertise SunPy and what SunPy can provide to support the eclipse that is occurring in April 2024. We could create a press release and share the link which would feature code examples and gallery examples. Some content already exists in the [solar-eclipse repo](https://github.com/sunpy/solar-eclipse). It needs to be updated and content migrated. This repo could then be made private or deleted.

Some additional content that could be added

- [ ] A recreation an eclipse path map such as [this one](https://svs.gsfc.nasa.gov/5073).
- [ ] A guide on how to import a photograph into a Map and determine its coordinates
- [ ] Example processing steps to be performed on an eclipse photograph like stacking, flat-fielding, and sharpening

However, `ArrayAnimatorWCS` provides no interface to this method. `ArrayAnimatorWCS.plot` should provide an input in order to adjust this, similar to what is done for `set_tick` through `plot_params`.

## PR Description

Modified the handling of axislabel in the coord_params dictionary.
Allowed axislabel to be a string or a dictionary.
If a dictionary is provided, it unpacks and passes the parameters to the set_axislabel method, enhancing flexibility for label customization.

PR Description

Modified the handling of axislabel in the coord_params dictionary.
Allowed axislabel to be a string or a dictionary.
If a dictionary is provided, it unpacks and passes the parameters to the set_axislabel method, enhancing flexibility for label customization.

Previously, the `axislabel` in `coord_params` only allowed passing a simple string to set the axis label.
With the new enhancement, axislabel can now accept a dictionary, which provides flexibility to include additional properties (e.g., color, font size, and other keyword arguments supported by `set_axislabel`).
line 181 added
```
if axislabel:
                if isinstance(axislabel, dict):
                   coord.set_axislabel(axislabel)
                else:
```
